title: ERC6551 execute with state mutation, ownership cycle detection, and self-own SELFDESTRUCT prevention
explanation: Three integrated mechanisms: (1) execute() does the standard call+returndata-bubble dance, then calls _updateState() which hashes (oldState || msg.data) as the new state — making every state-mutating call irreversibly observable in `state()`. The keccak256 length is rounded up to a 32-byte multiple via `and(add(0x7f, calldatasize()), not(0x1f))` to ensure deterministic hashing regardless of calldata length. (2) The 0x40 in memory at m+0x20 is the ABI offset for dynamic bytes — making the keccak input compatible with abi.encode(bytes32, bytes). (3) receiverFallback override checks ownership cycles on ERC721 receipt: walks the chain of token-bound owners via _hasOwnershipCycle(), reverting with SelfOwnDetected() if the cycle returns to address(this). Without this, an NFT could be transferred into its own ERC6551 account creating an inaccessible asset. ERC1155 receipt skips the check because batched transfers can't establish ownership cycles.
when_to_apply: ERC6551 token-bound accounts where state observability matters (governance, signature replay protection) and where NFT receipt could create ownership cycles
when_not_to_apply: Non-token-bound accounts (ERC4337), accounts that don't need observable state, or accounts that explicitly allow ownership cycles
